results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
newsletter icon

July 14th, 2026

# OpenAI wants your launches

## OpenAI wants your launches

Today: an AI agent gets its own debit card, a guard dog moves in with your database, and your trackpad learns to breathe. Plus $10k in OpenAI credits for whatever you build next.

## We teamed up with OpenAI

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.6, their largest model yet, and it's the first model in a while that has a lot of us opening a different terminal: trying a new harness, moving workflows over, comparing outputs. The labs leapfrogging each other is how everyone building on top ends up with better tools.

Aaron O'Leary

# Product Hunt 🤝 OpenAI

by

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

•

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.6, their largest model yet, and we're teaming up with them to get it into makers' hands.

Here's the deal:  
  
- Build something with Sol, Terra, or Luna  
- Schedule your launch on Product Hunt for July 23

- The top products walk away with $10k in OpenAI credits

If you've had an idea sitting in your notes app waiting for a model that could actually pull it off, this is the excuse. GPT-5.6 launched here just a few days ago and the early projects coming out of it are already impressive.

Drop what you're planning to build below and remember to schedule your launch 👀

186 views

### Replies

Best [...] Report

Mustafa Najoom

Bigger model, easier demo, harder production, not the other way around. GPT-5.6 will nail the flashy first pass, but you still have to decide what happens when it's confidently wrong and who approves what it actually does.

Report

Gal Dayan

worth flagging for anyone scheduling around this: every launch competing for the same $10k pool is also landing on Product Hunt the same day, so you're not just racing to build something good with GPT-5.6, you're racing every other GPT-5.6 launch for the same day's attention. might be worth staggering a day or two off July 23 if the goal is actually getting seen rather than qualifying for the prize

Report

Subscribe

results.title: OpenAI Forums on Product Hunt
results.content:
GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most advanced model yet, designed to handle real-world work with greater autonomy, speed, and efficiency. It excels at coding, research, data analysis, and task execution — planning, using tools, and iterating with minimal guidance — making it a powerful partner for complex, multi-step workflows.

123

•••

NextLast

Subscribe [...] I have been around since the GPT-3 days, and one thing I ve noticed with GPT-5 is how careful it has become.

One clear difference between GPT-5 and the earlier 4o model is a kind of hesitation when it comes to certain topics, be it macroeconomics, politics, or anything nuanced.

Aaron O'Leary•

7mo ago

### ChatGPT images vs Nano Banana

I ve been using Google s @NanoBanana image tools for a while now for quick visuals, edits, and the occasional cursed meme. They ve been good enough that I haven t really felt a big urge to switch.

But I m seeing a ton of buzz around @ChatGPT Images and how much better they are for real-world stuff like thumbnails, product shots, and UI mocks.

Rohan Chaubey•

2mo ago

### GPT-5.5 by OpenAI - OpenAI's smartest and most intuitive to use model yet

 GPT-5

  5.0 (32 reviews) [...] ## Top reviewed foundation models

Top reviewed

Across the most-reviewed foundation models, the field splits between broad application platforms, multimodal document/media handling, and open or portable options for privacy-conscious teams. OpenAI leads with APIs for coding, agents, voice, and automation; Gemini 2.5 stands out for long-context multimodal analysis; and Mistral AI emphasizes self-hosting, OCR, speech, and enterprise control.
